title: Other administrative occupations n.e.c.
description: All artists and related workers not listed separately. Play prerecorded music for live audiences at venues or events such as clubs, parties, or wedding receptions. May use techniques such as mixing, cutting, or sampling to manipulate recordings. May also perform as emcee (master of ceremonies). All entertainers and performers, sports and related workers not listed separately. (Based on 32 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Deliver completed work.
Maintain stock of supplies and requisition any needed items.
Clean machines, perform minor repairs, and report major repair needs.
Operate auxiliary machines such as collators, pad and tablet making machines, staplers, and paper punching, folding, cutting, and perforating machines.
Complete records of production, including work volumes and outputs, materials used, and any backlogs.
Sort or classify information according to guidelines, such as content, purpose, user criteria, or chronological, alphabetical, or numerical order.
Prepare and process papers for use in scanning, microfilming, and microfiche.
Clean and file master copies or plates.
Find, retrieve, and make copies of information from files in response to requests and deliver information to authorized users.
Keep records of materials filed or removed, using logbooks or computers, and generate computerized reports.
Perform general office activities, such as typing, answering telephones, operating office machines, processing mail, or securing confidential materials.
Eliminate outdated or unnecessary materials, destroying them or transferring them to inactive storage, according to file maintenance guidelines or legal requirements.
Modify or improve filing systems or implement new filing systems.
Answer questions about records or files.
File and store completed documents.
Gather materials to be filed from departments or employees.
Load machines with materials such as blank paper or film.
Read job orders to determine the type of work to be done, the quantities to be produced, and the materials needed.
Mark copy to indicate and correct errors in type, arrangement, grammar, punctuation, or spelling, using standard printers' marks.
Read corrected copies or proofs to ensure that all corrections have been made.
Compare information or figures on one record against same data on other records, or with original copy, to detect errors.
Consult reference books or secure aid of readers to check references with rules of grammar and composition.
Operate office machines such as high speed business photocopiers, scanners, addressing machines, microfilm readers or printers, and binder machines.
Operate electronic mail systems and coordinate the flow of information internally or with other organizations.
Greet visitors, log them in and out of the facility, assign them security badges, and contact employee escorts.
Answer incoming calls, greeting callers, providing information, transferring calls or taking messages as necessary.
Perform clerical duties such as typing, proofreading, and sorting mail.
Relay work orders, messages, or information to or from work crews, supervisors, or field inspectors, using telephones or two-way radios.
Conduct searches to find needed information, using such sources as the Internet.
Compute prices for services and receive payment, or provide supervisors with billing information.
Record messages, suggesting rewording for clarity or conciseness.
Perform various cash handling tasks, such as collecting payments, making bank deposits, or managing petty cash.
File and maintain records.

Transmit information or documents to customers, using computer, mail, or facsimile machine. Schedule appointments and maintain and update appointment calendars. Provide information about the establishment, such as location of departments or offices, employees within the organization, or services provided. Collect, sort, distribute, or prepare mail, messages, or courier deliveries. Process and prepare memos, correspondence, travel vouchers, or other documents. Maintain records pertaining to inventory, personnel, orders, supplies, or machine maintenance. Perform general office duties, such as ordering supplies, maintaining records management database systems, and performing basic bookkeeping work. Provide clerical support to other departments. Perform administrative support tasks, such as proofreading, transcribing handwritten information, or operating calculators or computers to work with pay records, invoices, balance sheets, or other documents. Answer phone calls and direct calls to appropriate parties or take messages. Greet visitors and determine whether they should be given access to specific individuals. Prepare responses to correspondence containing routine inquiries. Open, sort, and distribute incoming correspondence, including faxes and email. Maintain up-to-date knowledge of music releases and trends. Provide customer service, such as limited instructions on proceeding with claims or referrals to auto repair facilities or local contractors.

Keep a current record of staff members' whereabouts and availability. Schedule space or equipment for special programs and prepare lists of participants. Prepare and issue work schedules, deadlines, and duty assignments for office or administrative staff. Develop or update procedures, policies, or standards. Make recommendations to management concerning such issues as staffing decisions or procedural changes. Consult with managers or other personnel to resolve problems in areas such as equipment performance, output quality, or work schedules. Coordinate activities with other supervisory personnel or with other work units or departments. Discuss job performance problems with employees to identify causes and issues and to work on resolving problems. Provide employees with guidance in handling difficult or complex problems or in resolving escalated complaints or disputes. Supervise the work of office, administrative, or customer service employees to ensure adherence to quality standards, deadlines, and proper procedures, correcting errors or problems. Train or instruct employees in job duties or company policies or arrange for training to be provided.

Review records or reports pertaining to activities such as production, payroll, or shipping to verify details, monitor work activities, or evaluate performance. Research, compile, and prepare reports, manuals, correspondence, or other information required by management or governmental agencies. Conduct research, compile data, and prepare papers for consideration and presentation by executives, committees, and boards of directors. Attend meetings to record minutes. Prepare meeting agendas, attend meetings, and record and transcribe minutes.

Direct the work of wardrobe crews during dress rehearsals or performances. Provide dressing assistance to cast members or assign cast dressers to assist specific cast members with costume changes. Maintain, secure, or monitor inventories of costumes, wigs, or makeup, providing keys or access to assigned directors, costume designers, or wardrobe mistresses/masters. Examine costume fit on cast members and sketch or write notes for alterations. Clean and press costumes before and after performances and perform any minor repairs. Return borrowed or rented items when productions are complete and return other items to storage.

Create worksheets for dressing lists, show notes, or costume checks. Distribute costumes or related equipment and keep records of item status. Inventory stock to determine types or conditions of available costuming. Collaborate with production designers, costume designers, or other production staff to discuss and execute costume design details. Design or construct costumes or send them to tailors for construction, major repairs, or alterations. Purchase, rent, or requisition costumes or other wardrobe necessities. Check the appearance of costumes on stage or under lights to determine whether desired effects are being achieved.

Perform clerical tasks, such as answering phones or distributing mail. Document security transactions, such as purchases, sales, conversions, redemptions, or payments, using computers, accounting ledgers, or certificate records. Verify accuracy of transcripts by checking copies against original records of proceedings and accuracy of rulings by checking with judges. Transmit claims for payment or further investigation. Provide transcripts of proceedings upon request of judges, lawyers, or the public.

Type, format, proofread, and edit correspondence and other documents, from notes or dictating machines, using computers or typewriters. Prepare, maintain, and review purchasing files, reports and price lists. Check shipments when they arrive to ensure that orders have been filled correctly and that goods meet specifications. Review requisition orders to verify accuracy, terminology, and specifications. Prepare invoices, reports, memos, letters, financial statements, and other documents, using word processing, spreadsheet, database, or presentation software.

Compute figures such as balances, totals, or commissions. Analyze financial activities of establishments or departments and provide input into budget planning and preparation processes. Provide managers with budget recommendations and take responsibility for budgetary line items related to costumes, storage, or makeup needs. Participate in the hiring, training, scheduling, or supervision of alteration workers.

Monitor inventory levels and requisition or purchase supplies as needed. Arrange for necessary maintenance or repair work. Plan for or coordinate office services, such as equipment or supply acquisition or organization, disposal of assets, relocation, parking, maintenance, or security services.

- Accept music requests from event guests. - Adhere to schedules to keep events running on time. - Advertise services using media such as internet advertising and brochures. - Assemble audio and video equipment. - Collect payments from customers. - Communicate with clients or venue owners to determine event information, such as music preferences, scheduling, and anticipated attendance. - Conduct sound checks to ensure equipment is working and appropriate for the venue. - Create itemized invoices to record amounts due for services rendered. - Create tailored playlists by aligning music with event functions. - Develop written contracts for bookings. - Encourage guests to dance using group dances, competitions, or other party games. - Lead party games, such as dance-offs or prize giveaways. - Listen to music before playing at events to ensure recordings are appropriate and meet quality standards. - Mix, cut, or sample recorded music using DJ controllers, CDJs, or DJ mixers. - Operate disc jockey controller and other equipment, such as microphones. - Operate visual effects equipment, such as lights, fog machines, or lasers. - Organize music libraries or playlists. - Select and play music incorporating crowd preferences and mood.

Correspond with customers, including insurance agents, to obtain necessary information or inform them of changes and account status. Prepare correspondence, such as business letters and reports, and ensure the accuracy and completeness of documents.

Compile, review, and verify data, such as customer information and financial records, ensuring correctness before processing insurance applications, claims, or financial transactions. Maintain and update filing systems and databases for records of all transactions and account activities.

Communicate with customers to answer inquiries, take orders, provide information about services, and address complaints. This includes informing customers of procedures for services and issuing receipts for deposits and payments. Monitor and evaluate customer accounts.

Collect payments, process claims, and handle various financial transactions, including loan applications and credit assessments. This encompasses calculating amounts due, issuing receipts, and maintaining compliance with rules and regulations related to financial transactions.

Assemble and compile documentation for transactions, loans, and claims, ensuring compliance and accuracy. Review orders and correspondence for completeness and accuracy, and route information as necessary.

Prepare insurance claim forms or related documents, and review them for completeness and accuracy. Process, prepare, and submit necessary business or government forms, such as applications and claims while ensuring compliance with relevant regulations.

Maintain knowledge of relevant rules affecting financial services and inform staff or customers accordingly. This includes verifying ownership and records, monitoring inventory movement, and assisting in preparing financial reports.
File, type, and operate standard office machines while performing general office duties such as reviewing mail, preparing documents, and organizing material for further processing or response.

Contact suppliers and other organizations to gather product information or coordinate orders. Review supplier bills for accuracy and engage with vendors, maintaining effective communication for order fulfillment.

Monitor account statuses, resolve discrepancies, and perform checks to ensure the accuracy of financial records. This includes collecting data from various sources and maintaining organized documentation for auditing and compliance purposes.
Engage in basic bookkeeping activities such as collecting, counting, and disbursing money for transactions, ensuring all financial records are accurate and properly recorded and secured.

Assist in organizing materials for operational activities, including invoice preparation and submission of reports. Review documents for completeness and collaborate with team members to facilitate workflow as required.

Prepare and maintain loan records and related documentation to assist in the onboarding of clients regarding financial services. Follow up with customers on applications and loans to obtain further information or resolve issues as necessary.
Review correspondence for detail and accuracy, composing responses where necessary while directing requests and inquiries to appropriate departments as needed.
Confirm details from customers regarding orders, applications, and service inquiries, and maintain organized records of interactions and customer transactions.
Facilitate communications and transactions by coordinating schedules, preparing documentation for various departmental needs, and maintaining records necessary for compliance and auditing processes.
Assist in special projects that require compiling data or facilitating the communication of information while processing forms and documentation efficiently.

Compose and prepare letters, forms, contracts, and other documentation necessary to fulfill requests and maintain operational efficiency. This involves reviewing and verifying outgoing communications.
Complete and maintain detailed records of office activities, ensuring all tasks are logged appropriately for tracking and compliance, including the organization of various financial transactions.

Engage in fundraising activities such as scheduling events and securing necessary permits for operational engagement in various public events. Collaborate with external stakeholders or vendors for effective delivery of services.
Maintain strong relationships with clients through effective communication and problem-solving strategies to ensure satisfaction and retention of services.

Prepare cash for deposit or disbursement.

Search information sources to find specific data.

Discuss goods or services information with customers or patrons.

Review production information to determine costume or makeup requirements.

Test performance of electrical, electronic, mechanical, or integrated systems or equipment.

Estimate time or monetary resources needed to complete projects.
Evaluate quality of materials or products.
Design costumes or cosmetic effects for characters.
Arrange items for use or display.
Maintain records, documents, or other files.
Confer with clients to determine needs.
Manage budgets for personal services operations.

Review documents or materials for compliance with policies or regulations.

Process forensic or legal evidence in accordance with procedures.
Obtain information about goods or services.
Calculate weights, volumes or other characteristics of materials.
Determine the value of goods or services.
Correspond with customers to answer questions or resolve complaints.
Coordinate shipping activities with external parties.
Gather information about work conditions or locations.
Inspect items for damage or defects.
Inspect equipment to ensure safety or proper functioning.
Disassemble equipment for maintenance or repair.
Install electrical components, equipment, or systems.
Set up material handling gear or equipment, such as rigging, packaging, or temporary structures.
Program equipment to perform production tasks.
Collaborate with others to determine design specifications or details.

Design page layouts using text, photographs, graphics, and other elements.
Enter and update databases of contact information, such as names, addresses, and phone numbers.
Create patterns for costumes based on designer's drawings.
Perform secretarial tasks for the court.
